para_trans: E-Office System by Regional Offices under Revenue Department
The implementation of the VB regarding acceptance through the postal e-office system.
Government Circular No.: S. 2026 / P. No. / P. 019.
Martyr Rajguru Chowk, Madam Cama Marg,
Read - General Administration Department, Government Circular No. And why. ), D. February 02, 2023.

Instructions have been issued through the circular regarding the use of e-offos system in all administrative departments of the ministry as well as in the government work in field offices. Accordingly, work at the government level has started entirely through the e-offos system. However, mail is still being sent by hand-delivery as well as e-mail from the field agencies. With this, the following instructions are being issued regarding the mandatory use of e-offos system in the role of dynamic, transparent and people-oriented governance to achieve the goal of "Vikas Maharashtra-2047": - 1) All field level offices under the administrative head of Revenue and Forest Department should forward all revenue department-wise correspondence as well as presentation of office uniforms / wear through e-offos to the employees in the following table.
table_trans: Shri Ajit Deshmukh, Joint Secretary Konkan Division Shri Sanjay Dharurkar, Deputy Secretary and Smt. Manisha Jaibhaye, Joint Secretary Strategic and Legal Affairs Shri Sanjay Dharurkar, Deputy Secretary
Nagpur Division - Smt. Ashwini Yamgar, Deputy Secretary.
Nashik Division - Mr. Sanjay Bankar, Joint Secretary Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar - Mr. Kailash Gaikwad, Joint Secretary.

para_trans: 2) The district-wise reference under the Revenue Department by the above mentioned employees should be sent immediately through the e-office system to the Executive Officer and Supervisory Officer of the concerned Executive through e-offices system receipts.

3) In case of transfer of Executive Officer / Supervisory Officer of the concerned Executive, the information of the new incoming Executive Officer / Supervisory Officer will be communicated from the concerned Executive Officer to the field offices.

¥) in accordance with the above measures. All concerned should take appropriate note that no hand-delivered mail will be accepted at the government level after April 1, 2026, and the reference in the e-mail will not be treated as official mail.

5) In line with the above measures, the field offices will be provided with the required equipment (e.g., equipment, etc.) for the implementation of the e-offices system. Measures regarding availability of scanners, computers, tokens for digital signatures, etc.) should be taken immediately.

6) Training camps should be conducted by MahaIT / NIC offices at the local level or IT assistants should be hired from MahaIT / NIC available at the field office level if officers / staff need training in line with the use of e-office system.
This Government Circular is available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra www.maharashtra.gov.in.
table_trans: Court Cases / Cases, Ministry Control Room Related Posts, 9 Right to Information B Public Reference Commissioner Reference. Land Records Related Posts and Posts - Smt. Ashwini Yamgar, Deputy Secretary

Mr. Sanjay Bankar, Joint Joint Secretary and Madra Registration Registration and Stamp Department, Agriculture Corporation - Mr. Satyanarayan Bajaj, Joint Secretary to the Commissioner of Geometry and Deposit Control.
Public mail from Paavesam. Mr. Rajesh Bagade, Deputy Secretary
